terms.definition: A quantitative measure of disorder or micro-state multiplicity in a closed system, trending upward in the absence of energy input
The measurable alignment and mutual reinforcement among components of a system, enabling sustained ordered function
Any rule, resource limitation, or feedback mechanism that restricts available system micro-states and channels behavior
A boundary value of a control parameter beyond which the system undergoes a qualitative phase change
A discontinuous shift in macroscopic order arising from continuous variation in a control parameter (e.g., liquid to solid)
A measurable variable whose value distinguishes ordered from disordered phases in a system
The energetic, temporal, or resource expenditure required to maintain ordered behavior under given constraints
The regularity that reducing immediate cost increases deferred systemic entropy, yielding net negative outcomes
Macro-level alignment of norms, institutions, and behaviors that sustains collective functionality
An interdisciplinary field seeking consilience between theological concepts and physical-system modeling
